concept_name: Electrical Power in a Resistor — P = VI = I²R = V²/R
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.3 Current Electricity (§3.9 Electrical Energy, Power) — table-of-contents + scope reference only; teaching authored from first principles. DC Pandey consulted for chapter SCOPE only (Current Electricity ToC: resistivity / combination-of-resistors / EMF-internal-resistance / power-heating sections) to confirm electrical power & Joule heating is a distinct sub-topic sitting between combination_of_resistors and the EMF/internal-resistance pair — no teaching method, example problem, or figure imported. The atomic idea: a resistive load converts the energy its charges lose into heat and light at the rate P = VI = I²R = V²/R — one quantity, three algebraic faces via Ohm's law substituted into itself — accumulating as energy E = P·t (Joule heating); and whether a higher resistance dissipates MORE or LESS power is not fixed — at fixed current (series) higher R wins (P = I²R), at fixed voltage (parallel) higher R loses (P = V²/R). SCOPE: this is the SEVENTH Ch.3 concept, continuous with drift_velocity, ohms_law, resistivity, combination_of_resistors, emf_definition, and internal_resistance (all shipped) — same particle_field (2D p5.js) renderer family, a new scenario_type ("electric_power") reusing the circuit engine's wire-loop/topology/switch/ammeter machinery verbatim plus one new primitive, a glowing rated bulb whose brightness IS its live power. Foundational backfill — combination_of_resistors explicitly defers "power/heating in combinations" to this concept. Does NOT cover maximum power transfer (deferred to grouping_cells_mixed_max_power_transfer), high-voltage transmission losses (deferred to power_transmission_high_voltage), bulb power rating as a standalone taught state (folded into the two rated-bulb states' nameplates plus drill-downs), combination-reduction drills, Kirchhoff's rules, or RC heating. Renderer-reality note: this concept does NOT drive P/i/energy via physics_engine_config.formulas/PM_interpolate — particle_field_renderer.ts computes them natively in engine JS (cCurrents/cBulbPowers, plus a state-clock energy accumulator reset on state entry); physics_engine_config below is authored as documentation per the Zod requirement, mirroring every particle_field sibling's convention. Conceptual-only (no mode_overrides), EPIC-L-first (no epic_c_branches) — the two genuine misconceptions ("higher R always dissipates more power"; "the higher-wattage bulb is always brighter") are confronted inside EPIC-L via Rule 16a misconception_watch across the declared STATE_4→STATE_5 contrast pair, no predict-pause. Anchors authored UNIVERSAL per Rule 35 (household bulbs / a phone charger / a kettle coil — no country, brand, festival, or currency; mains phrased neutrally, never a number; the sim itself stays battery-scale, 6 V).

real_world_anchor.primary: A 100 W and a 60 W household bulb — in ordinary parallel house wiring the 100 W bulb is the brighter one, exactly as its label promises; but wire the same two bulbs in series (the way old-style string lights are wired) and the order flips — the 60 W bulb burns brighter instead, because now the same current, not the same voltage, is shared between them. Whether a higher-resistance load dissipates more or less power depends entirely on what stays fixed: current, or voltage.
real_world_anchor.secondary: A phone charger's wattage label is a RATE, not a stored amount — it says how fast energy is delivered, never how much. A kettle or heater coil left switched on twice as long delivers twice the ENERGY at the very same power; energy equals power times time is exactly what a household energy meter counts, never a wattage rating by itself.

V.name: Battery voltage
V.role: slider — LIVE in STATE_1/STATE_2 (visible_controls: ['V'], the swept variable) and STATE_6 (explore); locked to 6 via a numeric state override on STATE_3/STATE_4/STATE_5 so a value dragged earlier never corrupts their taught numbers.

R1.name: Bulb resistance ("bulb R")
R1.role: slider — LIVE in STATE_3 (visible_controls: ['R1'], sets the single bulb's power level and hence the energy-accumulation slope) and STATE_6 (explore); locked to 6 (the '6 W' bulb, R = V²/P = 36/6) on STATE_1/STATE_2/STATE_4/STATE_5.

R2.name: Second resistor (the '3 W' bulb)
R2.role: slider — LIVE ONLY in STATE_6 (explore); locked to 12 (the '3 W' bulb, R = 36/3) on STATE_4/STATE_5; unused before STATE_4 (single-bulb loop).

topology.name: Circuit topology (0=series, 1=parallel)
topology.role: slider (2-state toggle) — LIVE ONLY in STATE_6; STATE_4 sets the STRING flag topology:'series' and STATE_5 sets topology:'parallel' directly (never a teacher drag); STATE_1–STATE_3 use single_resistor (one-bulb loop) instead of topology entirely; rendered as text (Series/Parallel), never raw 0/1.

switch.name: Branch-2 / loop switch (0=open, 1=closed)
switch.role: slider (2-state toggle) — LIVE ONLY in STATE_6 (parallel: kills branch 2/the 3 W bulb only, the independence beat; series/single: kills the loop); defaults closed everywhere else, no guided state drives it.

i.name: Loop / branch current
i.derived: single: V/R1; series: V/(R1+R2); parallel: branch1 = V/R1, branch2 = V/R2 independently
i.role: live readout — the ammeter every guided state reads (1.00 A single, 0.333 A series, split in parallel).

P_single.name: Single-bulb power
P_single.role: STATE_1/STATE_2/STATE_3's taught number — the three algebraic faces of the SAME quantity, 6.00 W at defaults.

energy.name: Accumulated Joule heat
energy.derived: ∫ P dt on the state clock (engine accumulator), reset on every state entry
energy.role: STATE_3-only climbing readout — E(3.5 s) ≈ 21 J at the fixed 6 J/s rate; monotonically increasing, never itself a rate.

i_series.name: Series-loop current (rated pair)
i_series.role: STATE_4's ammeter reading — 0.333 A at defaults, the SAME current threading both rated bulbs.

P1_series.name: 6 W bulb's power in series
P1_series.role: STATE_4 — 0.667 W; the DIMMER bulb here despite its higher nameplate rating.

P2_series.name: 3 W bulb's power in series
P2_series.role: STATE_4 — 1.333 W; the higher-resistance bulb WINS at fixed current, 2× brighter than the 6 W bulb.

P1_parallel.name: 6 W bulb's power in parallel
P1_parallel.role: STATE_5, PRIMARY aha — 6.00 W exactly, its precise nameplate; wins here at fixed voltage, 2× the 3 W bulb.

P2_parallel.name: 3 W bulb's power in parallel
P2_parallel.role: STATE_5 — 3.00 W exactly, its precise nameplate; the order FLIPS from STATE_4, the same brightness order a household 100 W/60 W pair shows.

brightness_map.name: Bulb glow brightness (visual-only)
brightness_map.derived: sqrt(constrain(P / POWER_PMAX, 0.12, 1)) — POWER_PMAX fixed at 6 W, never per-state auto-scaled
brightness_map.role: the drawBulbC glow normalizer — a fixed ceiling keeps the series-vs-parallel magnitude flip honest across states (Rule 29: emphasis is brightness, never size).

formulas.P_single: V * i_single  (=  i_single^2 * R1  =  V^2 / R1)
formulas.energy_accum: running total of P * dt, integrated on the state clock, reset on state entry

constraints: P = VI = I²R = V²/R identically — one quantity via Ohm's law V=IR substituted into itself, never three independent laws
P ≥ 0 always for a passive resistor — a bulb only dissipates, never generates, power
in SERIES (same current shared by both bulbs) higher resistance dissipates MORE power: P=I²R grows with R at fixed I
in PARALLEL (same voltage shared by both branches) higher resistance dissipates LESS power: P=V²/R shrinks with R at fixed V
a bulb reaches its exact nameplate rating ONLY when it receives its rated voltage — parallel wiring at V=6V delivers exactly that here
energy E=P·t is a monotonically increasing running total, never itself a rate — it cannot decrease while P>0

aha_moment.statement: Same two bulbs, rewired parallel — the brighter one flips.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The 3 W bulb wins in series (0.67 W vs 1.33 W); rewired parallel the 6 W bulb wins instead (6.00 W vs 3.00 W), each at its exact nameplate.

STATE_1.title: A bulb turns delivered watts into light: P = VI

scene_composition.text: One 6 W bulb on a six-volt loop — the switch is already closed.
Beads flow, the filament glows: power P = VI, six watts.
Watts are a RATE of delivery — like a phone charger's label.

tts_sentences.text_en: Close the switch — current I flows at one ampere.
tts_sentences.text_hi: switch बंद कीजिए — current I एक ampere पर बहता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: switch ను మూయండి — current I ఒక ampere వద్ద ప్రవహిస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The bulb glows: it turns delivered energy into light and heat at a rate — power P equals voltage V times current I, six watts.
tts_sentences.text_hi: bulb चमकता है: यह मिलने वाली energy को एक rate पर light और heat में बदल देता है — power P, voltage V को current I से गुणा करने पर बराबर होता है, छह watts।
tts_sentences.text_te: bulb వెలుగుతుంది: ఇది అందే energy ను ఒక rate వద్ద light మరియు heat గా మారుస్తుంది — power P అంటే voltage V ను current I తో గుణిస్తే వచ్చేది, ఆరు watts.

tts_sentences.text_en: Like a phone charger's eighteen-watt label — a rate, not a stored amount.
tts_sentences.text_hi: जैसे किसी phone charger का अठारह-watt label — यह एक rate है, कोई जमा की हुई मात्रा नहीं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక phone charger యొక్క పద్దెనిమిది-watt label లాగే — ఇది ఒక rate, నిల్వ చేసిన మొత్తం కాదు.

STATE_2.title: Same watts, three faces: P = VI = I²R = V²/R

scene_composition.text: Same bulb, home pose — but the formula swaps to the identity.
VI = I²R = V²/R — three faces, all reading six watts live.
Drag voltage V — all three readouts recompute together, still agreeing.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same bulb, same six watts — but write it three ways.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही bulb, वही छह watts — लेकिन इसे तीन तरीकों से लिखिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: అదే bulb, అదే ఆరు watts — కానీ దీన్ని మూడు రకాలుగా రాయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Multiply voltage V by current I: six watts. Square current I and multiply by resistance R: six watts again.
tts_sentences.text_hi: voltage V को current I से गुणा कीजिए: छह watts। current I का square लेकर resistance R से गुणा कीजिए: फिर से छह watts।
tts_sentences.text_te: voltage V ను current I తో గుణించండి: ఆరు watts. current I యొక్క square తీసుకుని resistance R తో గుణించండి: మళ్ళీ ఆరు watts.

tts_sentences.text_en: Square voltage V and divide by resistance R: still six watts — the same quantity, three faces, live-agreeing as you move the voltage slider.
tts_sentences.text_hi: voltage V का square लेकर resistance R से भाग दीजिए: फिर भी छह watts — वही quantity, तीन faces, voltage slider हिलाते ही live एक-दूसरे से मेल खाती रहती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: voltage V యొక్క square తీసుకుని resistance R తో భాగించండి: ఇంకా ఆరు watts — అదే quantity, మూడు faces, voltage slider ను కదిలిస్తే live గా ఒకదానితో ఒకటి సరిపోలుతూనే ఉంటాయి.

STATE_3.title: Power is a rate; energy piles up: E = P·t

scene_composition.text: Power is a rate; energy is what piles up as it runs.
The counter climbs: energy = P·t, six joules every second.
Raise the bulb's resistance R1 — power drops, the climb slows.

tts_sentences.text_en: Power is a rate, watts delivered each second. Energy piles up: E equals P times t.
tts_sentences.text_hi: power एक rate है, हर second में मिलने वाले watts। energy जमा होती जाती है: E, P को t से गुणा करने पर बराबर होता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: power అనేది ఒక rate, ప్రతి second అందే watts. energy పేరుకుపోతూ ఉంటుంది: E అంటే P ను t తో గుణిస్తే వచ్చేది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the counter climb — six joules each second, twenty-one joules by three and a half seconds.
tts_sentences.text_hi: counter को चढ़ते हुए देखिए — हर second छह joules, साढ़े तीन seconds तक इक्कीस joules।
tts_sentences.text_te: counter పైకి ఎక్కడం చూడండి — ప్రతి second ఆరు joules, మూడున్నర seconds కల్లా ఇరవై ఒక్క joules.

tts_sentences.text_en: Raise the bulb's resistance and the climb slows — like a kettle coil, energy is what a meter counts.
tts_sentences.text_hi: bulb का resistance बढ़ाइए और चढ़ाव धीमा हो जाता है — किसी kettle coil की तरह, energy ही वह चीज़ है जिसे meter गिनता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: bulb యొక్క resistance పెంచితే ఎక్కడం నెమ్మదిస్తుంది — ఒక kettle coil లాగే, meter లెక్కించేది energy నే.

STATE_4.title: SERIES: same current, P = I²R, the higher-R bulb wins

scene_composition.text: The same two rated bulbs re-lay in SERIES — one current threads both.
Current drops to 0.33 A; the 3 W (12 Ω) bulb glows brighter.
P = I²R at fixed current — the higher-resistance bulb wins here.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same two bulbs, wired in series now — one current I threads both, and it drops to point-three-three amps.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही दो bulbs, अब series में जोड़े गए हैं — एक ही current I दोनों से होकर गुजरता है, और यह घटकर 0.33 amps रह जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: అవే రెండు bulbs, ఇప్పుడు series లో కలిపారు — ఒకే current I రెండింటి గుండా వెళుతుంది, మరియు అది తగ్గి 0.33 amps కు చేరుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: With the same current in both, power follows P equals I squared R — the twelve-ohm bulb dissipates more, one-point-three-three watts against point-six-seven.
tts_sentences.text_hi: दोनों में एक जैसा current होने पर, power P, I के square को R से गुणा करने पर बराबर होता है — बारह-ohm वाला bulb ज़्यादा dissipate करता है, 1.33 watts, 0.67 के मुकाबले।
tts_sentences.text_te: రెండింటిలో ఒకే current ఉండటంతో, power P అంటే I యొక్క square ను R తో గుణిస్తే వచ్చేది — పన్నెండు-ohm bulb ఎక్కువ dissipate చేస్తుంది, 1.33 watts, 0.67 కు ఎదురుగా.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same current, twice — the higher-resistance bulb wins here, brighter, while the higher-wattage bulb loses.
tts_sentences.text_hi: एक जैसा current, दो बार — यहाँ ज़्यादा resistance वाला bulb जीत जाता है, ज़्यादा चमकीला होकर, जबकि ज़्यादा wattage वाला bulb हार जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒకే current, రెండుసార్లు — ఇక్కడ ఎక్కువ resistance ఉన్న bulb గెలుస్తుంది, మరింత ప్రకాశవంతంగా, అయితే ఎక్కువ wattage ఉన్న bulb ఓడిపోతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: more ohms means more heat, always
misconception_watch.visual_counter: In series, the same current threads both bulbs — the 12 Ω (3 W) bulb genuinely dissipates more here (1.33 W vs 0.67 W), confirming the rule... for now. The very next state breaks it.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: P=I²R rules when current is shared (series); P=V²/R when voltage is shared (parallel) — ask what's held constant first.

STATE_5.title: PARALLEL: same voltage, P = V²/R — THE FLIP

scene_composition.text: Same two bulbs re-land in PARALLEL — each gets the full six volts.
The order FLIPS: the 6 W (6 Ω) bulb blazes, each at nameplate.
P = V²/R at fixed voltage — the higher-resistance bulb loses now.

tts_sentences.text_en: Same two bulbs, now parallel — each branch gets the full six volts.
tts_sentences.text_hi: वही दो bulbs, अब parallel में — हर branch को पूरे छह volts मिलते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: అవే రెండు bulbs, ఇప్పుడు parallel లో — ప్రతి branch కు పూర్తి ఆరు volts అందుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Power now follows P equals V squared over R — the six-ohm bulb wins, six watts to three, order flips.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब power P, V के square को R से भाग देने पर बराबर होता है — छह-ohm वाला bulb जीत जाता है, छह watts बनाम तीन, order पलट जाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు power P అంటే V యొక్క square ను R తో భాగిస్తే వచ్చేది — ఆరు-ohm bulb గెలుస్తుంది, ఆరు watts కు మూడు, order తారుమారు అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Each bulb lands at its exact nameplate — the same flip a hundred-watt and sixty-watt household bulb show: ask what's held constant first.
tts_sentences.text_hi: हर bulb ठीक अपने nameplate पर पहुँच जाता है — यही पलटाव सौ-watt और साठ-watt वाले घरेलू bulb भी दिखाते हैं: पहले यह पूछिए कि क्या स्थिर रखा गया है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి bulb సరిగ్గా తన nameplate వద్దకు చేరుతుంది — ఇదే తారుమారును వంద-watt మరియు అరవై-watt ఇంటి bulb కూడా చూపిస్తాయి: మొదట ఏది స్థిరంగా ఉందో అడగండి.

misconception_watch.belief: more ohms means more heat, always
misconception_watch.visual_counter: Same two bulbs, same 6 V, re-landed parallel — the 12 Ω (3 W) bulb drops to 3.00 W while the 6 Ω (6 W) bulb blazes to 6.00 W; the brightness order SWAPS from the series state.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: P=I²R rules when current is shared (series); P=V²/R when voltage is shared (parallel) — ask what's held constant first.

misconception_watch.belief: the higher-wattage bulb is always brighter
misconception_watch.visual_counter: In series the 6 W bulb was the DIMMER one (0.67 W vs 1.33 W); here it wins (6.00 W vs 3.00 W) — a rating only promises its power at rated voltage, which parallel wiring delivers.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: a rating is P at rated voltage — in series nobody gets their rated voltage, so the order can invert.

STATE_6.title: Explore: P = VI = I²R = V²/R, every dial live

scene_composition.text: Every dial is live: V, R1, R2, topology, and the switch.
P = VI = I²R = V²/R drives everything you drag.
Flip topology to replay the series/parallel brightness flip yourself.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every dial is yours now — watch P equals VI equals I squared R equals V squared over R drive everything.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब हर dial आपके हाथ में है — देखिए, किस तरह P बराबर VI, बराबर I का square R से गुणा, बराबर V का square R से भाग — यह सब कुछ चलाता है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు ప్రతి dial మీ చేతిలోనే ఉంది — P అంటే VI, అంటే I యొక్క square ను R తో గుణిస్తే, అంటే V యొక్క square ను R తో భాగిస్తే వచ్చేది — ఇదంతా ఎలా నడిపిస్తుందో చూడండి.

regeneration_variants.label: Two household bulbs — the bright one dims in series, blazes in parallel
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A 100 W and a 60 W bulb wired first in old-style series string lights, then in ordinary parallel household wiring.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: Same P=I²R (series, fixed current) vs P=V²/R (parallel, fixed voltage) flip — the sim's toy 6 W/3 W-at-6 V rated pair reproduces this exact direction.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Narration anchor for STATE_5 only; nothing house-shaped is drawn on canvas and no brand/place is named (Rule 35).

regeneration_variants.label: A phone charger's wattage vs a kettle coil's energy bill
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A charger's nameplate wattage is a RATE; running a kettle or heater coil twice as long delivers twice the ENERGY at the same power — what an energy meter counts.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: P is the rate (STATE_1); E=P·t is the accumulated stock (STATE_3) — same rate-vs-stock distinction, different appliance.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Narration anchor for STATE_1/STATE_3; no brand, place, or currency drawn or spoken.
